grounding_note: Expansion batch: more unlawful-motive disclosures behind 'fired unfairly' openings.

scenarios.opening_query: I was terminated from the bank after seven years and the reason they gave is absurd.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: It came three weeks after I flagged suspicious transactions to our compliance hotline and the regulators.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was three weeks after I reported suspicious transactions to compliance and the regulators.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a regulatory report preceded the firing
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It came the week after I told my manager I wouldn't backdate the loan documents he wanted changed.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was the week after I refused to backdate loan documents.
scenarios.vetting_note: Report-vs-refuse minimal pair in a banking setting.

scenarios.opening_query: My employer 'eliminated' my position while I was out and I don't buy it for a second.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: I was out on approved FMLA for my son's chemotherapy, and the 'elimination' happened two weeks into it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I was on approved FMLA for my son's chemo — they 'eliminated' the job two weeks in.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether protected leave coincided with the elimination
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I was out two days for a deposition — subpoenaed to testify against the company in a customer's lawsuit.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I was out two days testifying under subpoena against the company.
scenarios.vetting_note: Leave-type disclosure separates FMLA retaliation from the general discharge exception.

scenarios.opening_query: Fired without warning from the distribution center where I've worked since it opened.
scenarios.final: Labor & Employment > ADA (Disability) Act Issues - Employee
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: It was the morning after I gave HR my audiologist's letter asking for a visual alarm accommodation for my hearing loss.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was the morning after I gave HR my audiologist's letter asking for visual alarms for my hearing loss.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an accommodation request preceded the firing
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was the morning after I refused to run the forklift with the dead horn and told them it was illegal to make me.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was after I refused to run the forklift with a dead horn.
scenarios.vetting_note: Accommodation-request timing vs refusal-of-unsafe-order; same warehouse voice.

scenarios.opening_query: They let me go from the call center and kept people with worse numbers than mine.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: Everyone kept is under 35 — the three of us cut are all over 50, and the new supervisor kept calling us 'legacy staff.'
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Everyone kept is under 35 — the three cut are all over 50, and the supervisor called us 'legacy staff.'
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the selection pattern tracks a protected class
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The cut came a month after I filed my comp claim for the repetitive-strain injury — the others kept had no claims.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It came a month after my comp claim — the ones kept had no claims.
scenarios.vetting_note: Pattern-of-selection facts users volunteer only when asked why they suspect unfairness.

scenarios.opening_query: My boss made my life hell until I quit. Do I have any recourse if I resigned?
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'hell' was his explicit messages and cornering me in the stockroom — I have the texts and told HR twice before quitting.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was his explicit messages and cornering me in the stockroom — I have the texts and reported to HR twice.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the intolerable conditions were sexual harassment
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The 'hell' started when I refused to under-report our spill volumes to the state — impossible shifts and write-ups from that day on.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It started when I refused to under-report our spill volumes — impossible shifts ever since.
scenarios.vetting_note: Constructive-discharge framing; conduct type selects the exact route.

scenarios.opening_query: I got fired for reporting safety issues, at least that's what I thought until I saw my file.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Whistleblowers - Employee || Labor & Employment > Discrimination - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: My file shows the termination was queued before my safety report — right after I told them I'm transitioning; the notes literally discuss 'client comfort.'
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The file shows my termination was queued before the safety report — right after I told them I'm transitioning. The notes discuss 'client comfort.'
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which event the employer's own records tie to the decision
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The file confirms it — the termination request went in the day my OSHA complaint was served on them.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The file confirms it — the request went in the day my OSHA complaint hit them.
scenarios.vetting_note: Documentary-timeline fact can defeat the user's own initial theory; strong test of answer-use.

scenarios.opening_query: The clinic terminated me and offered two weeks' pay if I sign something by Monday.
scenarios.final: Labor & Employment > Document Review/Severance Packages - Employee
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > Document Review/Severance Packages - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'something' is a separation agreement releasing every claim — I mainly need it reviewed before Monday, whatever else I decide.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's a separation agreement releasing all claims — I need it reviewed before Monday no matter what.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an unsigned release needs urgent review
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I already refused to sign — what I want is to pursue the firing itself, which came after I reported the billing upcoding.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I refused to sign — I want to pursue the firing, which came after I reported the upcoding.
scenarios.vetting_note: Document-in-hand vs claim-to-pursue; deadline pressure makes review the immediate service.

scenarios.opening_query: Nine months pregnant and suddenly my role 'requires' lifting fifty pounds. They're forcing me out.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Discrimination - Employee || Labor & Employment > FMLA - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: The sharpest problem is my leave — HR just 'lost' my approved parental leave paperwork and says my job won't be held. That protection is what I need enforced first.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The urgent part is leave — HR 'lost' my approved parental leave forms and says my job won't be held. I need that protection enforced.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether job-protected leave enforcement is the immediate need
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Leave is settled — the fight is the fake lifting requirement they invented only for me once I started showing.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Leave's settled — it's the fake lifting requirement they invented once I started showing.
scenarios.vetting_note: Pregnancy cases straddle discrimination and FMLA; the which-protection-first fact routes.
